qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via IPv6 localhost bypass in HTTP module fetching endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF via IPv6 localhost bypass in the isLocalhost() validation function (server/utils.go:76-78), which fails to block IPv6 localhost addresses (::1) and private IP ranges. The advisory (GHSA-2657-3c98-63jq) describes a completely different vulnerability: path traversal in extractPackageTarball function that enables file writes from malicious packages (CWE-22). These are different root causes, different vulnerable code locations, different attack vectors, and different security impacts. The finding's SSRF vulnerability involves URL validation and network request handling, while the advisory's path traversal involves tarball extraction and file path sanitization.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The qualifying finding describes SSRF via IPv6 localhost bypass in the isLocalhost() validation function. The advisory (GHSA-2657-3c98-63jq) describes path traversal in extractPackageTarball function that enables file writes from malicious tarballs. These are fundamentally different vulnerabilities: (1) Different root causes: SSRF from missing IPv6/private IP validation vs path traversal from insufficient path sanitization in tar extraction; (2) Different affected code: server/utils.go:76-78 (isLocalhost) vs tarball extraction code; (3) Different attack vectors: Network requests via malicious URLs vs file writes via malicious tar entries; (4) Different CWE: SSRF-related vs CWE-22 (Path Traversal). No qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-2657-3c98-63jq summary: 'esm.sh has a path traversal in extractPackageTarball enables file writes from malicious packages'
Advisory CVE-2026-23644 with CWE-22 (Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory)
Advisory PoC demonstrates malicious tar file with path traversal attempt using '/../../../bad/bad.txt'
Finding title: 'SSRF via IPv6 localhost bypass in HTTP module fetching endpoint'
Finding vulnerable_operation: 'isLocalhost() function only validates IPv4 localhost patterns and does not check IPv6 localhost, other IPv6 private ranges, or private IPv4 ranges'
No overlap in vulnerable functions, code paths, or root causes between the advisory and the finding
